q: What was Jesus' first miracle?
e: John 2:11 — This, the first of his signs, Jesus performed at Cana in Galilee.

q: How many people did Jesus feed with five loaves and two fish?
e: Matthew 14:21 — The number of those who ate was about five thousand men, besides women and children.

q: Who walked on water with Jesus?
e: Matthew 14:29 — Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water and came toward Jesus.

q: How many people were raised from the dead in the Bible?
e: Various — Elijah, Elisha, Jesus, Peter, and Paul all performed or were involved in resurrections.

q: Which miracle did Elisha perform for the widow?
e: 2 Kings 4:1-7 — Elisha multiplied the widow's oil to pay her debts.

q: What happened when Jesus touched the ear of the high priest's servant?
e: Luke 22:51 — And he touched his ear and healed him.

q: How many years had the woman with the issue of blood been suffering?
e: Luke 8:43 — And a woman was there who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years.

q: Who was raised from the dead after being in the tomb for four days?
e: John 11:39 — 'By this time there is a bad odor, for he has been there four days.'

q: What did Jesus use to heal the blind man at Bethsaida?
e: Mark 8:23 — He spit on the man’s eyes and put his hands on him.

q: How many lepers did Jesus heal at once?
e: Luke 17:12-14 — Ten men who had leprosy met him.

q: How many of the ten healed lepers returned to thank Jesus?
e: Luke 17:15-18 — One of them, when he saw he was healed, came back...

q: What did Peter find in the mouth of a fish to pay the temple tax?
e: Matthew 17:27 — Take the first fish you catch; open its mouth and you will find a four-drachma coin.

q: Whose mother-in-law did Jesus heal of a fever?
e: Matthew 8:14-15 — Jesus saw Peter’s mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever... she got up and began to wait on him.

q: What did Jesus say to the storm on the Sea of Galilee?
e: Mark 4:39 — He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, 'Quiet! Be still!'

q: How many baskets of leftovers were collected after feeding the 5,000?
e: Matthew 14:20 — They picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

q: Which prophet called down fire from heaven to consume a sacrifice?
e: 1 Kings 18:38 — Then the fire of the Lord fell and burned up the sacrifice.

q: What happened to the sun during the battle at Gibeon?
e: Joshua 10:13 — The sun stopped in the middle of the sky and delayed going down about a full day.

q: What miracle did Peter perform on Aeneas?
e: Acts 9:33-34 — 'Aeneas,' Peter said to him, 'Jesus Christ heals you. Get up.'

q: Who was restored to life by Peter in Joppa?
e: Acts 9:40 — Peter sent them all out of the room; then he got down on his knees and prayed. Turning toward the dead woman, he said, 'Tabitha, get up.'

q: How did Paul survive the bite of a poisonous viper on Malta?
o: He shook it off into the fire and suffered no ill effects
e: Acts 28:5 — But Paul shook the snake off into the fire and suffered no ill effects.

q: What miracle happened at the pool of Bethesda?
o: An angel stirred the water for healing
e: John 5:4 — From time to time an angel of the Lord would come down and stir up the waters.

q: Who was healed of leprosy after washing in the Jordan River seven times?
e: 2 Kings 5:14 — So he went down and dipped himself in the Jordan seven times... and his flesh was restored.

q: What did Elisha do to make the iron ax head float?
o: He threw a stick into the water
He touched it with his staff
e: 2 Kings 6:6 — Elisha cut a stick and threw it there, and made the iron float.

q: What miracle did Jesus perform for the Centurion's servant?
e: Matthew 8:13 — Then Jesus said to the centurion, 'Go! Let it be done just as you believed it would.' And his servant was healed at that moment.

q: What was the name of the man Jesus healed of many demons, who lived in the tombs?
e: Mark 5:9 — Then Jesus asked him, 'What is your name?' 'My name is Legion,' he replied, 'for we are many.'

q: How many people were fed with seven loaves and a few small fish?
e: Matthew 15:38 — The number of those who ate was four thousand men, besides women and children.

q: Which miracle did Jesus perform on the Sabbath that angered the Pharisees?
o: Healed a man with a shriveled hand
e: Matthew 12:10-13 — Then he said to the man, 'Stretch out your hand.' He stretched it out and it was completely restored.

q: How did Jesus heal the man born blind in John 9?
o: Spit on the ground, made mud, and put it on his eyes
e: John 9:6 — He spit on the ground, made some mud with the saliva, and put it on the man’s eyes.

q: What did the disciples think they saw when Jesus was walking on the water?
e: Matthew 14:26 — When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified. 'It’s a ghost,' they said.

q: Whose ear was cut off and then healed by Jesus in the garden?
e: John 18:10 — The servant’s name was Malchus. (Luke 22:51 mentions the healing).

q: What did Elisha do to the Shunammite woman's son?
e: 2 Kings 4:32-35 — Elisha went in, shut the door... and the boy sneezed seven times and opened his eyes.

q: How was the shadow on the sundial moved back ten steps for King Hezekiah?
e: 2 Kings 20:11 — Then the prophet Isaiah called on the Lord, and the Lord made the shadow go back.

q: What miracle happened when the Israelites carried the Ark into the Jordan River?
e: Joshua 3:15-16 — As soon as the priests who carried the ark reached the Jordan... the water from upstream stopped flowing.

q: How did God provide water for the Israelites in the desert at Rephidim?
e: Exodus 17:6 — Strike the rock, and water will come out of it for the people to drink.

q: What miracle did Paul perform in Lystra?
o: Healed a man lame from birth
Raised a man from the dead
e: Acts 14:8-10 — Paul looked at him... and called out, 'Stand up on your feet!' At that, the man jumped up and began to walk.

q: Who was blinded on the road to Damascus by a great light?
e: Acts 9:3-9 — A light from heaven flashed around him... For three days he was blind.

q: What miracle happened to the prison doors while Paul and Silas were singing?
o: They were opened by an angel
e: Acts 16:26 — Suddenly there was such a violent earthquake that the foundations of the prison were shaken. At once all the prison doors flew open.

q: How did Elisha heal the poisonous stew?
e: 2 Kings 4:41 — Elisha said, 'Get some flour.' He put it into the pot... and there was nothing harmful in the pot.

q: What did the prophet Isaiah use to heal King Hezekiah's boil?
e: 2 Kings 20:7 — Then Isaiah said, 'Prepare a poultice of figs.' They did so and applied it to the boil, and he recovered.

q: What miracle did Elisha perform on the waters of Jericho?
e: 2 Kings 2:21 — Then he went out to the spring and threw salt into it... 'I have healed this water.'

q: How many times did Elijah stretch himself over the widow's son to raise him?
e: 1 Kings 17:21 — Then he stretched himself out on the boy three times and cried out to the Lord.

q: What happened to the Egyptian chariots in the Red Sea before the water fell?
e: Exodus 14:25 — He jammed the wheels of their chariots so that they had difficulty driving.

q: Who was healed of leprosy after questioning Moses' authority?
e: Numbers 12:10 — When the cloud lifted from above the tent, Miriam's skin was leprous.

q: What miracle did God perform to show He had chosen Aaron?
o: His staff budded and produced almonds
e: Numbers 17:8 — The next day Moses entered the tent... and Aaron’s staff... had not only sprouted but had budded, blossomed and produced almonds.

q: How was the wall of Jericho destroyed?
o: The people shouted and the wall fell
e: Joshua 6:20 — When the trumpets sounded, the army shouted, and at the sound of the trumpet, when the men gave a loud shout, the wall collapsed.

q: What miracle happened when the dead man's body touched Elisha's bones?
o: The man came back to life
The man was healed of leprosy
e: 2 Kings 13:21 — When the body touched Elisha’s bones, the man came to life and stood up on his feet.

q: How did Jesus heal the man lowered through the roof?
o: Said his sins were forgiven and told him to walk
e: Mark 2:5-12 — 'Son, your sins are forgiven... Get up, take your mat and go home.'

q: What was the result of Jesus cursing the fig tree?
e: Matthew 21:19 — Immediately the tree withered.

q: Who was healed of blindness after Paul prayed for him in Malta?
e: Acts 28:8 — His father was sick in bed... Paul went in to see him and, after prayer, placed his hands on him and healed him.

q: What miracle occurred when Peter and John met the lame man at the Beautiful Gate?
o: He was healed and began to walk and leap
He was given a new mat
He was raised from the dead
e: Acts 3:6-8 — 'In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk.'... He jumped to his feet and began to walk.
